declaration: Phase L design artifacts comply with all applicable Canon Laws and Rules. No production code was written. All protocols, models, and integration blueprints are pure design documents.

laws.title: Shared Models Outside Runtime
laws.law_ref: LAW 6 — All shared data types must be defined outside the runtime core
laws.evidence_detail: All 10+ data models (MemoryLayer, ContextWindow, SkillNode, ForgettingPolicy, MemoryEntry, etc.) are defined in the design models file outside core/. No dataclass imports or references any runtime-internal type (ExecutionEvent, DAG, Engine). The models file is purely abstract — no runtime side effects, no imports from core/.
covered_items: MemoryLayer enum (4 layers, all outside runtime)
MemoryEntry dataclass (internal storage unit, documented for clarity)
Zero runtime imports in models file

laws.title: Recoverability
laws.law_ref: LAW 8 — Every operation must be logged and recoverable via trace ID
laws.evidence_detail: All three protocols (IMemoryHierarchy, IContextCompiler, ISkillGraphManager) require cognitive_trace_id on every method. Every dataclass in the models file carries cognitive_trace_id. The integration blueprint (04_integration_blueprint.md §2) defines the backward trace resolution chain from ContextWindow._hash → cognitive_trace_id → enterprise_trace_id → F1 trace_id, enabling full recovery of any memory operation.
covered_items: EventBus hooks for observability events (MemoryPruneTriggered, ContextOverflow, SkillMismatch, TenantIsolationEnforced)
Rollback conditions defined in integration blueprint (§4)

laws.title: Enterprise Isolation
laws.law_ref: LAW 11 — All tenant operations must carry tenant_id and enforce isolation
laws.evidence_detail: Every memory store/retrieve/prune operation is scoped by tenant_id. The state machine defines G-M1 (Tenant Match) as the gate for every memory transition. The memory consistency matrix (§2) lists cross-tenant context leak prevention as the first concern, enforced by tenant_id filtering on every retrieval and G-M3 (scope_verified) for any cross-tenant access. The TenantIsolationEnforcedReport model captures every blocked isolation violation.
covered_items: G-M1 Tenant Match guard on all transitions
G-M3 Scope Verified guard for cross-tenant access
Memory consistency matrix explicitly prevents cross-tenant leakage

laws.title: Deterministic Retrieval
laws.law_ref: LAW 14 — Context retrieval must be deterministic for same inputs
laws.evidence_detail: Section 3 defines the Deterministic Retrieval Table, listing all input components with their determinism guarantees and the Context Drift Prevention mechanism. ContextWindow._hash is computed from all fields via SHA-256; same (trace_id, tenant_id, max_tokens, scope_verified) inputs always produce the same hash. TokenBudget reservations are validated in __post_init__ to prevent silent clipping drift. PruneDecision determinism is guaranteed via G-M6.
covered_items: Deterministic Retrieval Table with all 7 input components
Context Drift Prevention mechanism with example calls

laws.title: Tenant Context Isolation
laws.law_ref: LAW 15 — Compiled context must never leak cross-tenant data
laws.evidence_detail: IContextCompiler.validate_boundary_safety() is the dedicated enforcement point for LAW 15. It verifies that a compiled ContextWindow contains no cross-tenant data and returns a structured violation report. SafetyBounds dataclass locks the isolation contract with a _safety_hash. Every protocol method's retrieve and prune operations are tenant-scoped. The integration blueprint (§4) sets cross-tenant context leakage threshold to absolute zero.
covered_items: Zero cross-tenant leakage threshold in acceptance criteria
scope_verified flag required for cross-tenant compilation

rules.title: No Cross-Layer Imports
rules.rule_ref: RULE 1 — No layer may import from another layer's implementation
rules.evidence_detail: All three protocols define their interfaces without importing from core/ or any other layer. The models file explicitly states 'No runtime-internal types (ExecutionEvent, DAG, Engine) appear in any model.' The architectural boundaries diagram in 04_integration_blueprint.md (§5) shows Phase L as a separate block communicating only through EventBus and EmoRuntimeFacade protocol methods — no cross-layer imports.
covered_items: Protocols import only from typing and phase_l models
Models import only from stdlib (dataclasses, enum, hashlib, etc.)
Architectural boundary diagram shows Phase L as isolated block
Zero core/ imports in any design file

rules.title: All Systems Require Interfaces
rules.rule_ref: RULE 2 — Every subsystem must be defined through a formal Protocol
rules.evidence_detail: Three runtime_checkable Protocols are defined: IMemoryHierarchy, IContextCompiler, and ISkillGraphManager. Each has 4 methods with full typed signatures, docstrings, and return schemas. They follow the same convention as the Phase J2 enterprise protocols (01_enterprise_protocols.py). All return Dict[str, Any] and accept cognitive_trace_id for audit.
covered_items: IMemoryHierarchy protocol (4 methods: store, retrieve, prune, get_context_window)
IContextCompiler protocol (3 methods: compress, inject, validate)
ISkillGraphManager protocol (4 methods: record_plan, retrieve_skill, update_weight, record_failure)

rules.title: Replay Safety
rules.rule_ref: RULE 3 — All operations must be replay-safe and deterministic
rules.evidence_detail: Replay safety is guaranteed at multiple levels: (1) ContextWindow._hash is deterministic, (2) PruneDecision is deterministic per policy (G-M6), (3) Memory consistency matrix lists 'Context drift' as explicitly prevented by hash comparison, (4) ForgettingPolicy.__post_init__ computes a _policy_hash locking the policy parameters, (5) The integration blueprint sets 'Context hash match rate >= 99.9%' as a threshold.

stop_conditions.condition: Any memory model missing tenant_id or isolation_policy
stop_conditions.evidence: Every MemoryEntry, ContextWindow, SkillNode, ForgettingPolicy, and FailurePattern carries tenant_id

stop_conditions.condition: ContextCompiler allows cross-tenant context without scope_verified
stop_conditions.evidence: IContextCompiler.compress_trace_to_context() requires scope_verified: bool = False; validate_boundary_safety() enforces isolation

stop_conditions.condition: Integration blueprint incompatible with LAW 8 (Recoverability) or RULE 4 (Deterministic Wiring)
stop_conditions.evidence: Blueprint defines backward trace resolution chain, latency budgets, and rollback conditions. All integration is through EventBus and EmoRuntimeFacade protocol methods.

stop_conditions.condition: Any production code written in core/
stop_conditions.evidence: All 5 files are under artifacts/design/phase_l/. Zero files modified in core/ or tests/.
